# Pace Zones & Training Intensity

## Overview

Pace zones define the appropriate running speed for each training type. Zones are typically derived from recent race results, threshold testing, or estimated V̇O₂ max.

## Primary Training Zones

### Easy Pace (Z1)
- **Usage:** Easy runs, recovery sessions, warm-ups, cool-downs
- **Effort:** Conversational, RPE 4-5/10
- **Heart Rate:** 60-70% of max HR
- **Characteristics:** Should feel sustainable for hours

### Threshold Pace (Z2)
- **Usage:** Lactate threshold workouts, tempo runs
- **Effort:** Challenging but sustainable, RPE 7-8/10
- **Heart Rate:** 85-90% of max HR
- **Duration:** 20-40 minutes total (may include intervals)

### Long Run Pace (Z3)
- **Usage:** Saturday long runs during build phase
- **Effort:** Steady aerobic, RPE 5-6/10
- **Heart Rate:** 75-85% of max HR
- **Note:** Should sustain for 1.5-3+ hours

### VO₂ Max Pace (Z4)
- **Usage:** High-intensity intervals, speed work
- **Effort:** Hard, RPE 8-9/10
- **Heart Rate:** 90-95% of max HR
- **Duration:** Usually shorter intervals (3-8 min repeats)

---

## Finding Your Zones

### From Recent Marathon Goal
- **Marathon Pace** = goal race pace (e.g., 5:40/km for sub-4h)
- **Long Run Pace** ≈ Marathon Pace + 30-45 sec/km
- **Threshold Pace** ≈ Marathon Pace - 15-30 sec/km
- **Easy Pace** ≈ Marathon Pace + 60-90 sec/km

### Example
For a **sub-4h marathon goal** (5:40/km):
- Easy Pace: ~7:00-7:30/km
- Long Run Pace: ~6:15-6:30/km
- Marathon Goals: 5:40/km
- Threshold Pace: 5:15-5:30/km
- VO₂ Max: ~4:50-5:10/km

---

## Adjusting Zones Over Time

As fitness improves, zones shift:
- Test every 6-8 weeks
- Update based on recent time trials or race results
- Adjust when athlete reports sustained changes in effort

## Reference

See [Corrida e Performance channel](https://www.youtube.com/@CorridaePerformance) for detailed zone-setting methodology.
